what_it_does: Provides a root CSS entry point that imports colors_and_type.css and documents that consumers should link this stylesheet instead of individual source sheets.
observations: Contains a large banner comment explaining the stylesheet's role.
States that app- and kit-specific chrome are intentionally not imported here.
Shows an example link tag for consumers.
standouts: The file is documentation-heavy relative to its single CSS rule.
It separates the public entry point from implementation-specific surface styles.
conventions: Uses a structured banner comment to describe purpose and usage.
Keeps the root stylesheet narrowly scoped to shared design-system inputs.
concerns: The entry point depends entirely on colors_and_type.css being present and loadable.
No other imports or fallback styles are defined in this file.
notes: This is the consumer-facing root stylesheet for pulling in the shared design-system CSS layer.
questions: Are additional source-of-truth sheets expected to be imported here later, or is colors_and_type.css the complete public payload?
Should @import be used here instead of direct bundling or link tags in consuming surfaces?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file claims to be a single consumer entry point and its actual CSS only imports the shared colors/type stylesheet while excluding app-specific chrome.
apparent_themes: separation of shared primitives from surface chrome
intention_signals: It seems intended to give consumers one stable stylesheet to link while keeping app and kit implementation styles outside the shared design-system entry point.
novelty: A root stylesheet whose functional content is a single import, with most of the file devoted to boundary-setting documentation.
contribution: Contributes a neutral public CSS entry layer that gathers shared design-system styling behind one import path and documents which surface-specific styles stay outside that layer.
summary_for_embedding: A root ConceptV design-system stylesheet that imports colors_and_type.css and documents itself as the single consumer entry point for shared tokens, type presets, and surface-zone styling.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to simplify stylesheet consumption while preserving a boundary between shared design-system CSS and app- or kit-specific chrome.
